title: Bounty Piliers
sources: README.md: Role definitions and meritocratic bounty philosophy
quests.ts: Foundational quest specifications for District E
database.sql: Schema for the memos table and role-based permissions
forom-config.ts: Category E (Piliers) and Action 2 (Quête) labels
App.tsx: Quest reward (2 PX) and internal economy redistribution
resume: The quest 'Bounty Piliers' (E2) represents the active deployment of the community's core thematic anchors within the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid. Located at the intersection of District E (Piliers) and the 'Quête' action (2), this quest is the functional mechanism that transforms abstract values into actionable tasks. In Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), the mission to 'Save human communities and long-term memory' requires more than just ideation and exchange; it demands the creation of specific bounties that incentivize members to build the content and tools that embody these pillars. This quest is a 'Call to Action' for the community to identify what tangible work is needed to solidify the node's thematic identity. Technically, these pillar-related bounties are archived in the local PostgreSQL database within the 'memos' table, ensuring that the history of the community's active work remains sovereign and private. The Forom architecture, built with React 19 and Vite 7, allows members to navigate these active quests through a spatial 5x5 viewport, providing a clear visual representation of where the community's energy is being directed. This local hosting on hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson ensures that the community's roadmap for thematic growth is protected from centralized corporate surveillance, adhering to the 'Minecraft Server' philosophy. Economically, completing the E2 quest rewards the contributor with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP, supporting the redistribution of purchasing power within the node. This incentive structure is vital for encouraging the 'Creator' role (Yellow alignment) to produce high-quality foundational memos that align with the community's core mission. For pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, E2 is the tool that ensures thematic consistency by turning club values into specific milestones for members to achieve. Every entry at coordinate E2 becomes a permanent part of the V1 Archive, set to be refined in V2 and finalized in V3. Once the community reaches Phase 3, these 'Bounty Piliers' will be ingested by ROM, the local RAG engine, to help the AI understand how the community prioritizes action based on its values. Ultimately, E2 transforms the community's pillars into a dynamic, actionable engine for growth, securing the node's structural and cultural integrity for the decade ahead. This is the finalized coordinate for active thematic development. Mission secure. The systematic archival of these bounties ensures that every contribution is recognized and that the collective memory of the node's efforts is preserved for all future members. Yes.
summary: This quest formalizes the creation of thematic bounties at coordinate E2, transforming core pillars into actionable tasks to build the node's sovereign foundational content.
